blCoverage.BL-GQL-002: covered — all 3 cases reference it; no [PERF] assertion in 050c (perf owned by 050g XCC-GQL-018)

blCoverage.BL-GQL-001: partially covered — ORD-GQL-013 implicitly exercises error contract; not listed in Business_Rule

ORD-GQL-013-decision.rationale: Test design is correct. Both coupons and currency.name are present in the live introspected schema. The test legitimately probes valid schema fields. Runtime INVALID_OPERATION errors are production resolver bugs, not test defects. Schema-coverage canary tests are most valuable when they stay RED until the bug is fixed — Option B defers the bug visibility, Option C hides it entirely.
requiredFollowUp: File JIRA bug: CustomerOrderType.coupons resolver INVALID_OPERATION
File JIRA bug: CurrencyType.name resolver INVALID_OPERATION
Do NOT remove or guard coupons/currency.name selections from ORD-GQL-013

recommendation: Option A — keep schema-coverage probe as-is; file two JIRA bugs for resolver failures (coupons + currency.name); add JIRA ticket refs to all three cases' References column
verdictRationale: Zero Blockers, zero Criticals, zero Lows. Three High findings are all the same REQ-001 pattern (missing JIRA ticket in References) — editorial, non-blocking for regression. No auto-fixes required (Cleanup column was already correctly populated). ORD-GQL-013 confirmed as correctly authored schema-canary; its regression failures are production bugs not test defects.
